{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "General Manager",
  "description": "OneVERIFY Limited is a leading Nigerian technology company specializing in digital identity, verification, RegTech, AI, cybersecurity, and blockchain-based solutions. We build platforms that enable secure identity verification, AML/KYC compliance, fraud prevention, and data integrity for both government and private sector clients. Our people are our greatest asset, and we prioritize fostering a high-performance, inclusive, and compliant organizational culture.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: General Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Garki, Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The General Manager (GM) will provide strategic and operational leadership across all functions of OneVERIFY acting as a key driver of business performance, process&nbsp;optimization, and cross-functional coordination.
	The GM will work closely with&nbsp;executive leadership to ensure operational excellence, alignment with organizational&nbsp;goals, and growth across technical, commercial, and administrative areas.


Key Responsibilities
Strategic Leadership:


	Oversee the execution of company strategy, aligning operations, finance, HR, and technology initiatives.
	Translate business goals into actionable operational plans with measurable outcomes.
	Support the executive team in long-term planning and decision-making.


Operations &amp; Project Oversight:


	Ensure the smooth operation of all departments including tech, product, operations, HR, and finance.
	Monitor project timelines, deliverables, and budgets to ensure accountability and efficiency.
	Implement best practices for workflow, process automation, and operational scalability.


Team &amp; People Management:


	Lead, mentor, and motivate departmental heads and teams to achieve&nbsp;objectives.
	Foster a high-performance culture that promotes collaboration, accountability, and professional growth.
	Identify talent gaps and support recruitment and succession planning initiatives.


Financial &amp; Commercial Management:


	Support budgeting, forecasting, and financial reporting.
	Monitor resource allocation and operational expenses to optimizeefficiency.
	Collaborate with business development teams to ensure alignment of commercial goals and operational capacity.


Compliance &amp; Risk Management:


	Ensure all operations comply with regulatory, legal, and data protection requirements.
	Identify operational risks and implement mitigation strategies.
	Maintain standards for data security, privacy, and system integrity.


Reporting &amp; Analytics:


	Provide comprehensive reports on operational performance, KPIs, and strategic initiatives.
	Analyze metrics to identify opportunities for improvement and growth.
	Support data-driven decision-making across the organization.


Required Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Management, Engineering, or related field (Master&rsquo;s preferred).
	7&ndash;10 years of experience in general management or senior&nbsp;leadership roles in tech, fintech, or SaaS companies.
	Strong understanding of digital identity, verification, RegTech, and technology- driven operations.
	Proven ability to manage multiple departments, projects, and teams effectively.
	Excellent leadership, strategic thinking, and decision-making skills.
	Proficiency in operational, project management, and reporting tools.


Preferred Skills / Nice to Have:


	Experience in digital identity, verification platforms, or AI-driven products.
	Knowledge of Nigerian regulatory and compliance frameworks for data and&nbsp;technology.
	Familiarity with hybrid and remote team management.
	Exposure to blockchain, cybersecurity, or RegTech solutions.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send&nbsp;their CV / Cover Letter to:&nbsp;careers@oneverify.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

General Manager Job at OneVERIFY Limited




        
    

        
        OneVERIFY Limited is a leading Nigerian technology company specializing in digital identity, verification, RegTech, AI, cybersecurity, and blockchain-based solutions. We build platforms that enable secure identity verification, AML/KYC compliance, fraud prevention, and data integrity for both government and private sector clients. Our people are our greatest asset, and we prioritize fostering a high-performance, inclusive, and compliant organizational culture.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: General Manager

Job Location: Garki, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The General Manager (GM) will provide strategic and operational leadership across all functions of OneVERIFY acting as a key driver of business performance, process optimization, and cross-functional coordination.
	The GM will work closely with executive leadership to ensure operational excellence, alignment with organizational goals, and growth across technical, commercial, and administrative areas.


Key Responsibilities
Strategic Leadership:


	Oversee the execution of company strategy, aligning operations, finance, HR, and technology initiatives.
	Translate business goals into actionable operational plans with measurable outcomes.
	Support the executive team in long-term planning and decision-making.


Operations & Project Oversight:


	Ensure the smooth operation of all departments including tech, product, operations, HR, and finance.
	Monitor project timelines, deliverables, and budgets to ensure accountability and efficiency.
	Implement best practices for workflow, process automation, and operational scalability.


Team & People Management:


	Lead, mentor, and motivate departmental heads and teams to achieve objectives.
	Foster a high-performance culture that promotes collaboration, accountability, and professional growth.
	Identify talent gaps and support recruitment and succession planning initiatives.


Financial & Commercial Management:


	Support budgeting, forecasting, and financial reporting.
	Monitor resource allocation and operational expenses to optimizeefficiency.
	Collaborate with business development teams to ensure alignment of commercial goals and operational capacity.


Compliance & Risk Management:


	Ensure all operations comply with regulatory, legal, and data protection requirements.
	Identify operational risks and implement mitigation strategies.
	Maintain standards for data security, privacy, and system integrity.


Reporting & Analytics:


	Provide comprehensive reports on operational performance, KPIs, and strategic initiatives.
	Analyze metrics to identify opportunities for improvement and growth.
	Support data-driven decision-making across the organization.


Required Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Management, Engineering, or related field (Master’s preferred).
	7–10 years of experience in general management or senior leadership roles in tech, fintech, or SaaS companies.
	Strong understanding of digital identity, verification, RegTech, and technology- driven operations.
	Proven ability to manage multiple departments, projects, and teams effectively.
	Excellent leadership, strategic thinking, and decision-making skills.
	Proficiency in operational, project management, and reporting tools.


Preferred Skills / Nice to Have:


	Experience in digital identity, verification platforms, or AI-driven products.
	Knowledge of Nigerian regulatory and compliance frameworks for data and technology.
	Familiarity with hybrid and remote team management.
	Exposure to blockchain, cybersecurity, or RegTech solutions.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV / Cover Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Cashier",
  "description": "Custom Gifts &amp; Designs is a reputable business platform that promotes consumer access to premium gift packaging services and quality retail supplies. Our platform is propagated via partnerships and proven proprietary business systems that assure a holistic shopping experience for every occasion.Together with everyone in our ecosystem, we continue to develop our iconic CGD&trade; brand by inspiring stronger social ties with our novelty gifts offering.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Cashier

Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Cashier is responsible for accurately processing, documenting, and reporting all sales transactions. This role ensures financial accuracy, maintains controlled access to daily orders, and strictly adheres to company policies and procedures.


Responsibilities


	Write and print accurate customer invoices.
	Process all sales transactions strictly using the company ERP system.
	Enter all daily sales transactions into the official Google Sheet.
	Maintain and manage Daily Orders.
	Serve as the only unit authorized to access Daily Orders.
	Ensure all financial records are accurate, complete, and submitted daily.
	Maintain strict confidentiality of sales and financial information.
	Work strictly according to assigned shift schedules.
	Comply with the company rule of no permanent shift for any staff.


Educational Qualifications


	
	Minimum qualification: SSCE (Senior Secondary Certificate of Education)
	


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong numerical and calculation skills.
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail.
	Integrity and ability to handle confidential information.
	Basic computer proficiency: ERP systems, Google Sheets, and email.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet daily deadlines.


Experience


	
	Previous experience in cashiering, accounting, or financial record-keeping is preferred but not mandatory.
	


Other Requirements:


	Willingness to work on a rotational shift basis.
	High level of honesty, discipline, and accountability.
	Ability to strictly follow company policies and procedures.


Remuneration &amp; Benefits


	Salary:&nbsp;N100,000 &ndash; N120,000 per month
	HMO (Health Maintenance Organization) coverage
	Staff bus transportation
	Accommodation provided
	Performance bonus when revenue targets are met
	Other benefits as per company policy.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV to:&nbsp;cgdbrandmanager@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Cashier Job at Custom Gifts & Designs




        
    

        
        Custom Gifts & Designs is a reputable business platform that promotes consumer access to premium gift packaging services and quality retail supplies. Our platform is propagated via partnerships and proven proprietary business systems that assure a holistic shopping experience for every occasion.Together with everyone in our ecosystem, we continue to develop our iconic CGD™ brand by inspiring stronger social ties with our novelty gifts offering.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Cashier

Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Cashier is responsible for accurately processing, documenting, and reporting all sales transactions. This role ensures financial accuracy, maintains controlled access to daily orders, and strictly adheres to company policies and procedures.


Responsibilities


	Write and print accurate customer invoices.
	Process all sales transactions strictly using the company ERP system.
	Enter all daily sales transactions into the official Google Sheet.
	Maintain and manage Daily Orders.
	Serve as the only unit authorized to access Daily Orders.
	Ensure all financial records are accurate, complete, and submitted daily.
	Maintain strict confidentiality of sales and financial information.
	Work strictly according to assigned shift schedules.
	Comply with the company rule of no permanent shift for any staff.


Educational Qualifications


	
	Minimum qualification: SSCE (Senior Secondary Certificate of Education)
	


Skills & Competencies:


	Strong numerical and calculation skills.
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail.
	Integrity and ability to handle confidential information.
	Basic computer proficiency: ERP systems, Google Sheets, and email.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet daily deadlines.


Experience


	
	Previous experience in cashiering, accounting, or financial record-keeping is preferred but not mandatory.
	


Other Requirements:


	Willingness to work on a rotational shift basis.
	High level of honesty, discipline, and accountability.
	Ability to strictly follow company policies and procedures.


Remuneration & Benefits


	Salary: N100,000 – N120,000 per month
	HMO (Health Maintenance Organization) coverage
	Staff bus transportation
	Accommodation provided
	Performance bonus when revenue targets are met
	Other benefits as per company policy.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Account Officer",
  "description": "Peridot Forte Solutions Consulting&nbsp;-&nbsp;Our client in the Accounting Consulting industry is&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;&nbsp;Lekki,&nbsp;Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time
Work Type: Hybrid

Job Summary


	The Account Officer will be responsible for supporting day-to-day accounting operations, maintaining accurate financial records, assisting with audit and compliance engagements, and providing accounting support to multiple client accounts.
	The role requires strong attention to detail, sound accounting knowledge, and the ability to work in a professional consulting environment.


Responsibilities


	Prepare and maintain accurate accounting records, ledgers, and supporting schedules for client accounts.
	Assist in the preparation of financial statements in line with applicable accounting standards.
	Support audit assignments by preparing working papers, schedules, and required documentation.
	Handle bank reconciliations, journal entries, expense tracking, and account reconciliations.
	Assist with tax computations, filings, and statutory compliance requirements.
	Support payroll processing and related statutory deductions where required.
	Liaise with clients to obtain financial information and resolve accounting queries.
	Ensure proper documentation and filing of financial records for audit and compliance purposes.
	Support senior accountants and audit managers on consulting and assurance engagements.
	Maintain confidentiality and uphold professional and ethical accounting standards.


Requirements


	First degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of 1&ndash;2 years of relevant experience in an accounting firm, consulting firm, or audit environment.
	Sound knowledge of basic accounting principles and financial reporting.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software (e.g. Sage, QuickBooks, Tally, or similar).
	Strong attention to detail and analytical skills.
	Good communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work with clients and internal teams.
	Ability to work independently and manage multiple client tasks effectively.


Method of Application
Interested candidates should send CVs to:&nbsp;peridotforte@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job&nbsp;Position as the subject of the email.",

Account Officer Job at Peridot Forte Solutions Consulting




        
    

        
        Peridot Forte Solutions Consulting - Our client in the Accounting Consulting industry is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Officer

Job Location:  Lekki, Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time
Work Type: Hybrid

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Account Officer will be responsible for supporting day-to-day accounting operations, maintaining accurate financial records, assisting with audit and compliance engagements, and providing accounting support to multiple client accounts.
	The role requires strong attention to detail, sound accounting knowledge, and the ability to work in a professional consulting environment.


Responsibilities


	Prepare and maintain accurate accounting records, ledgers, and supporting schedules for client accounts.
	Assist in the preparation of financial statements in line with applicable accounting standards.
	Support audit assignments by preparing working papers, schedules, and required documentation.
	Handle bank reconciliations, journal entries, expense tracking, and account reconciliations.
	Assist with tax computations, filings, and statutory compliance requirements.
	Support payroll processing and related statutory deductions where required.
	Liaise with clients to obtain financial information and resolve accounting queries.
	Ensure proper documentation and filing of financial records for audit and compliance purposes.
	Support senior accountants and audit managers on consulting and assurance engagements.
	Maintain confidentiality and uphold professional and ethical accounting standards.


Requirements


	First degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of 1–2 years of relevant experience in an accounting firm, consulting firm, or audit environment.
	Sound knowledge of basic accounting principles and financial reporting.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software (e.g. Sage, QuickBooks, Tally, or similar).
	Strong attention to detail and analytical skills.
	Good communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work with clients and internal teams.
	Ability to work independently and manage multiple client tasks effectively.


Method of Application
Interested candidates should send CVs to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "IT Operations Personnel",
  "description": "Ibifoundry Limited is a business services and digital strategy firm.&nbsp;The company specializes in several key professional areas:&nbsp;Business Systems: Designing and implementing structured operational frameworks for organizations,&nbsp;Digital Strategy: Developing online growth and marketing plans to enhance brand presence,&nbsp;Enterprise-Level Growth: Providing expert guidance on scaling businesses and improving overall enterprise performance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;IT Operations Personnel

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time+

Responsibilities


	Maintain and optimize local company networks and servers.
	Be responsible for device and password management.
	Oversee data backup and system security operations (e.g. user authorization, firewalls).
	Manage installations, upgrades and configurations of hardware and software.
	Assess system performance and recommend improvements.
	Resolve issues escalated by technicians and engineers.
	Ensure data is handled, transferred or processed according to legal and company guidelines.
	Provide support and guidance to stakeholders via help desk.
	Control costs and budgets regarding IT systems.
	Manage contracts with vendors (e.g. development platforms, telecommunication companies, password managers) and software licenses.
	Develop IT policies and practices.


Requirements and Skills


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications.
	Proven experience as IT Operations Manager.
	Experience with system installation, configuration and analysis.
	Thorough knowledge of networks and cloud computing.
	Knowledge of data protection operations and legislation (e.g. GDPR).
	Leadership and organizational skills.
	Ability to manage multiple projects.
	Outstanding communication skills.
	Problem-solving aptitude.


Salary
N85,000 - N200,000 / month.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to HR via:&nbsp;ibifoundrylimited@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  },

IT Operations Personnel Job at Ibifoundry Limited




        
    

        
        Ibifoundry Limited is a business services and digital strategy firm. The company specializes in several key professional areas: Business Systems: Designing and implementing structured operational frameworks for organizations, Digital Strategy: Developing online growth and marketing plans to enhance brand presence, Enterprise-Level Growth: Providing expert guidance on scaling businesses and improving overall enterprise performance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: IT Operations Personnel

Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time+

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Maintain and optimize local company networks and servers.
	Be responsible for device and password management.
	Oversee data backup and system security operations (e.g. user authorization, firewalls).
	Manage installations, upgrades and configurations of hardware and software.
	Assess system performance and recommend improvements.
	Resolve issues escalated by technicians and engineers.
	Ensure data is handled, transferred or processed according to legal and company guidelines.
	Provide support and guidance to stakeholders via help desk.
	Control costs and budgets regarding IT systems.
	Manage contracts with vendors (e.g. development platforms, telecommunication companies, password managers) and software licenses.
	Develop IT policies and practices.


Requirements and Skills


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications.
	Proven experience as IT Operations Manager.
	Experience with system installation, configuration and analysis.
	Thorough knowledge of networks and cloud computing.
	Knowledge of data protection operations and legislation (e.g. GDPR).
	Leadership and organizational skills.
	Ability to manage multiple projects.
	Outstanding communication skills.
	Problem-solving aptitude.


Salary
N85,000 - N200,000 / month. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to HR via: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

title: Job Openings at Credible Eye Care
content:
Credible Eye Care is an optical clinic that promises world-class eye care to all our patients. Helping you keep your vision healthy is our goal.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Marketing Executive

Job Location: Ojodu Berger, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Field marketing 4 times a week
	Visiting hospitals, labs, and organizations for potential partnerships
	Creating strategy to improve sales


Requirements / Skills
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Interested candidates should possess an HND qualification with 1 - 2 years of working experience.
	Must able to communicate effectively
	Must be proactive and be strategic.


Salary
N130,000 - N145,000 / month.

Application Closing Date: 29th March, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail. 

Note


	Applicant must reside close to job location (Ojodu Berger).
	Basic salary is not based on target.


 

2.) Social Media / Digital Marketing Officer

Job Location: Festac, Lagos
Employment Type: Part-time

Description


	making and editing videos
	creating graphic designs
	managing the social media handles
	Transforming contents into sales


Skills:


	Must know how to use the various tools required for good content delivery
	must know how to generate good ideas and contents that will promote sales.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 per month.

Application Closing Date: 10th March, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail. 

Note: The staff is expected to work at the office 5 days a week

 

3.) Front Desk Staff / Customer Service Officer

Job Location: Ago, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Welcoming patients.
	Sending reports.
	Responding to phone calls/ whatsapp messages / mails.
	Sending Hmo request.
	Sending daily sales report.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications with 0 - 2 years of experience.
	Must reside close to, or within the work area environment.


Skill:


	Must be diligent.
	Must be proactive.
	Must have the ability to multitask.
	Must have good communication skill.


Salary
N100,000 - N130,000 per month.

Application Closing Date: 16th March, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.     

    

        Click here to Apply
